message.content: Now generating all 21 scenario YAML files:

function.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_sys_reset_clock.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_sys_reset_clock
description: >
  Verifies the system-level reset and clock architecture of mspm33c321a_derivative.
  Covers power-on reset (POR) hold behaviour, external nRESET pad assertion,
  software reset (SYSRESETREQ), 80 MHz clock startup, and the WWDT-to-chip-reset
  propagation path.
checklist_ids:
  - SYS-01
  - SYS-02
  - SYS-03
  - SYS-04
  - SYS-05
test_names:
  - test_sys_por_state
  - test_sys_external_reset
  - test_sys_sw_reset
  - test_sys_clock_freq
  - test_sys_wwdt_reset_path
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5       # 80 MHz

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:
  - name: jtag_probe_model
    type: gpio_stimulus
    connect:
      stimulus_out: nreset_pad
    params:
      pulse_width_ns: 500
      assert_polarity: low

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: assert_reset
    description: Assert reset_n for 20 clock cycles to establish POR state
  - step: 2
    action: check_peripheral_reset
    description: Verify all peripheral registers hold reset values while reset_n is asserted
  - step: 3
    action: deassert_reset
    description: Release reset_n; verify compute_ss begins normal operation
  - step: 4
    action: drive_gpio_stimulus
    device: jtag_probe_model
    description: Pulse nRESET pad low via gpio_stimulus model; verify reset_n propagates
  - step: 5
    action: write_csr
    target: SYSRESETREQ_REG
    value: 0x1
    description: Write SYSRESETREQ to trigger software reset
  - step: 6
    action: wait_cycles
    cycles: 50
    description: Wait for WWDT timeout path (fast-timeout mode) and observe chip reset

checks:
  - id: CHK-SYS-01
    checklist_id: SYS-01
    description: All peripheral CSRs read back reset values during POR hold
    expected: All registers at documented reset values
  - id: CHK-SYS-02
    checklist_id: SYS-02
    description: nRESET pad assertion drives internal reset_n low within 2 clock cycles
    expected: reset_n asserted within 2 cycles of pad assertion
  - id: CHK-SYS-03
    checklist_id: SYS-03
    description: SYSRESETREQ write causes compute_ss reset; CPU re-fetches from 0x00000000
    expected: PC returns to reset vector after SYSRESETREQ
  - id: CHK-SYS-04
    checklist_id: SYS-04
    description: Clock measurement confirms 80 MHz ± 0.5% after reset release
    expected: clk period = 12.5 ns ± 63 ps
  - id: CHK-SYS-05
    checklist_id: SYS-05
    description: WWDT expiry asserts internal reset within specified timeout window
    expected: reset_n re-asserted within WWDT timeout period

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-SYS checks PASS
  - No unexpected X-states on reset_n or clk during test
  - Simulation completes without timeout
"}
function.name: str_replace_editor

extras.new_content:
scenario_name: scenario_sys_reset_clock
description: >
  Verifies the system-level reset and clock architecture of mspm33c321a_derivative.
  Covers power-on reset (POR) hold behaviour, external nRESET pad assertion,
  software reset (SYSRESETREQ), 80 MHz clock startup, and the WWDT-to-chip-reset
  propagation path.
checklist_ids:
  - SYS-01
  - SYS-02
  - SYS-03
  - SYS-04
  - SYS-05
test_names:
  - test_sys_por_state
  - test_sys_external_reset
  - test_sys_sw_reset
  - test_sys_clock_freq
  - test_sys_wwdt_reset_path
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5       # 80 MHz

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices:
  - name: jtag_probe_model
    type: gpio_stimulus
    connect:
      stimulus_out: nreset_pad
    params:
      pulse_width_ns: 500
      assert_polarity: low

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: assert_reset
    description: Assert reset_n for 20 clock cycles to establish POR state
  - step: 2
    action: check_peripheral_reset
    description: Verify all peripheral registers hold reset values while reset_n is asserted
  - step: 3
    action: deassert_reset
    description: Release reset_n; verify compute_ss begins normal operation
  - step: 4
    action: drive_gpio_stimulus
    device: jtag_probe_model
    description: Pulse nRESET pad low via gpio_stimulus model; verify reset_n propagates
  - step: 5
    action: write_csr
    target: SYSRESETREQ_REG
    value: 0x1
    description: Write SYSRESETREQ to trigger software reset
  - step: 6
    action: wait_cycles
    cycles: 50
    description: Wait for WWDT timeout path (fast-timeout mode) and observe chip reset

checks:
  - id: CHK-SYS-01
    checklist_id: SYS-01
    description: All peripheral CSRs read back reset values during POR hold
    expected: All registers at documented reset values
  - id: CHK-SYS-02
    checklist_id: SYS-02
    description: nRESET pad assertion drives internal reset_n low within 2 clock cycles
    expected: reset_n asserted within 2 cycles of pad assertion
  - id: CHK-SYS-03
    checklist_id: SYS-03
    description: SYSRESETREQ write causes compute_ss reset; CPU re-fetches from 0x00000000
    expected: PC returns to reset vector after SYSRESETREQ
  - id: CHK-SYS-04
    checklist_id: SYS-04
    description: Clock measurement confirms 80 MHz ± 0.5% after reset release
    expected: clk period = 12.5 ns ± 63 ps
  - id: CHK-SYS-05
    checklist_id: SYS-05
    description: WWDT expiry asserts internal reset within specified timeout window
    expected: reset_n re-asserted within WWDT timeout period

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-SYS checks PASS
  - No unexpected X-states on reset_n or clk during test
  - Simulation completes without timeout
